bibliographic_data.title: Nonseparable Space-Time Stationary Covariance Functions on Networks cross Time
bibliographic_data.abstract: The advent of data science has provided an increasing number of challenges with high data complexity. This paper addresses the challenge of space-time data where the spatial domain is not a planar surface, a sphere, or a linear network, but a generalized network (termed a graph with Euclidean edges). Additionally, data are repeatedly measured over different temporal instants. We provide new classes of nonseparable space-time stationary covariance functions where space can be a generalized network, a Euclidean tree, or a linear network, and where time can be linear or circular (seasonal). Because the construction principles are technical, we focus on illustrations that guide the reader through the construction of statistically interpretable examples. A simulation study demonstrates that we can recover the correct model when compared to misspecified models. In addition, our simulation studies show that we effectively recover simulation parameters. In our data analysis, we consider a traffic accident dataset that shows improved model performance based on covariance specifications and network-based metrics.

core_content.novelty_claim: The paper provides new classes of nonseparable space-time stationary covariance functions for generalized networks, Euclidean trees, or linear networks with time being either linear or circular.
core_content.key_methodology: The authors describe a procedure to compose two parametric classes of functions describing spatial and temporal dependence. This combination results in the new class of nonseparable space-time covariance functions. The paper also considers covariance functions that are dynamically compactly supported over generalized networks cross linear or circular time, which allows for important computational gains.
outcomes: A simulation study demonstrates correct model recovery when compared to misspecified models and effective parameter recovery.
The analysis of a traffic accident dataset shows improved model performance based on covariance specifications and network-based metrics.
core_content.full_text_markdown: This paper addresses the challenge of space-time data where the spatial domain is not a planar surface, a sphere, or a linear network but a generalized network (termed a graph with Euclidean edges). The authors provide new classes of nonseparable space-time stationary covariance functions for such networks and time being either linear or circular. They describe a procedure to compose two parametric classes of functions describing spatial and temporal dependence, resulting in the new class of nonseparable space-time covariance functions. Additionally, they consider covariance functions that are dynamically compactly supported over generalized networks cross linear or circular time, which allows for important computational gains. A simulation study demonstrates correct model recovery when compared to misspecified models and effective parameter recovery. The analysis of a traffic accident dataset shows improved model performance based on covariance specifications and network-based metrics.
